Warren Buffett understands how to harness the power of unity. Having created billions of dollars of wealth for his investors, the global financial media have frequently rated him as the most successful investor of his time. While addressing shareholders of Berkshire Hathaway, the company he leads, Buffett regularly and sincerely uses words and messages that draw attention to unity by speaking of the Berkshire Hathaway family of investors. In fact, in one important report to shareholders, he engaged the idea of family in a novel way before recommending that investors continue to purchase Berkshire stock. He said, although nothing is certain, I will tell you what I would say to my own family today about Berkshire's future. Only then did he launch into a list of the reasons he thought would cause investors to believe in Berkshire's ongoing success. It was a brilliant statement that convinced shareholders by giving them the same information he would give to his close kin, which made them feel even more a part of the Berkshire family.
